```{r}
caffeine_feeds_binned_plot <- ggplot(binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "CAF"),],
                                      aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = number_feedingattempts, color = Solution, shape = Solution)) +
  # Scatter plot with jitter for individual points
  geom_point(aes(color = Solution, shape = Solution), alpha = 0.5,  position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0)) + 
  geom_ribbon(data = mean_data_caffeine_feeds, inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(x = midpoint_hrs, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = Solution),
              alpha = 0.4) +
  # Line connecting the mean values across time bins
  geom_line(data = mean_data_caffeine_feeds, aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = mean_fed, color = Solution), size = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "Caffeine" = "#FFAC1C")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "Caffeine" = "#FFAC1C")) +
  labs(x = "Time segment (h)",
       y = "Total feeding duration (s)",
       title = "") +
  theme_minimal() + scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22), labels = c("0â€“4", "4â€“8", "8â€“12", "12-16", "16-20", "20-24")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(), legend.position = "none", 
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold",)) 

caffeine_feeds_binned_plot
```

```{r}
mod_caffeine_feeds <- glmmTMB(number_feedingattempts~Solution * as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "CAF"),])

simres <- simulateResiduals(mod_caffeine_feeds)
plot(simres)
testDispersion(simres)
testZeroInflation(simres) 
car::Anova(mod_caffeine_feeds)

mod_caffeine_feeds_red <- glmmTMB(number_feedingattempts~Solution + as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "CAF"),])
car::Anova(mod_caffeine_feeds_red)

emmb <- emmeans(mod_caffeine_feeds, ~ Solution|Time_bin)
contrast(emmb, method = "pairwise", adjust = "bonferroni")
```


## TMX

```{r}
# Summarize the mean for each Solution in each time bin, filtering for Nic_choice
mean_data_TMX_duration <- binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "TMX"),] %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_fed = mean(total_duration, na.rm = TRUE),
                   se = sd(total_duration, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
                   lower = mean_fed - se,
                   upper = mean_fed + se,
                   .groups = "drop")
mean_data_TMX_feeds <- binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "TMX"),] %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_fed = mean(number_feedingattempts, na.rm = TRUE),
                   se = sd(number_feedingattempts, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
                   lower = mean_fed - se,
                   upper = mean_fed + se,
                   .groups = "drop")

mean_data_TMX_duration$midpoint_hrs <- c(2, 2, 6, 6, 10, 10, 14, 14, 18, 18, 22, 22)
mean_data_TMX_feeds$midpoint_hrs <- c(2, 2, 6, 6, 10, 10, 14, 14, 18, 18, 22, 22)
```

### Duration

```{r}
# Create the plot
TMX_duration_binned_plot <- ggplot(binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "TMX"),],
                                      aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = total_duration, color = Solution, shape = Solution)) +
  # Scatter plot with jitter for individual points
  geom_point(aes(color = Solution, shape = Solution), alpha = 0.5,  position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0)) + 
  geom_ribbon(data = mean_data_TMX_duration, inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(x = midpoint_hrs, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = Solution),
              alpha = 0.4) +
  # Line connecting the mean values across time bins
  geom_line(data = mean_data_TMX_duration, aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = mean_fed, color = Solution), size = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "TMX" = "darkgreen")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "TMX" = "darkgreen")) +
  labs(x = "Time segment (h)",
       y = "Total feeding duration (s)",
       title = "") +
  theme_minimal() + scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22), labels = c("0â€“4", "4â€“8", "8â€“12", "12-16", "16-20", "20-24")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(), legend.position = "none", 
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold",)) + ylim(c(0, 300))

TMX_duration_binned_plot
```

```{r}
mod_TMX_duration <- glmmTMB(total_duration~Solution * as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "TMX"),])

simres <- simulateResiduals(mod_TMX_duration)
plot(simres)
testDispersion(simres)
testZeroInflation(simres) 
car::Anova(mod_TMX_duration)

mod_TMX_duration_red <- glmmTMB(total_duration~Solution + as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "TMX"),])
car::Anova(mod_TMX_duration_red)

emmb <- emmeans(mod_TMX_duration, ~ Solution|Time_bin)
contrast(emmb, method = "revpairwise", adjust = "bonferroni")
```

### Feeds

```{r}
# Create the plot
TMX_feeds_binned_plot <- ggplot(binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "TMX"),],
                                      aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = number_feedingattempts, color = Solution, shape = Solution)) +
  # Scatter plot with jitter for individual points
  geom_point(aes(color = Solution, shape = Solution), alpha = 0.5,  position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0)) + 
  geom_ribbon(data = mean_data_TMX_feeds, inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(x = midpoint_hrs, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = Solution),
              alpha = 0.4) +
  # Line connecting the mean values across time bins
  geom_line(data = mean_data_TMX_feeds, aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = mean_fed, color = Solution), size = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "TMX" = "darkgreen")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "TMX" = "darkgreen")) +
  labs(x = "Time segment (h)",
       y = "Total feeding duration (s)",
       title = "") +
  theme_minimal() + scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22), labels = c("0â€“4", "4â€“8", "8â€“12", "12-16", "16-20", "20-24")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(), legend.position = "none", 
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold",)) 

TMX_feeds_binned_plot
```

```{r}
mod_TMX_feeds <- glmmTMB(number_feedingattempts~Solution * as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "TMX"),])

simres <- simulateResiduals(mod_TMX_feeds)
plot(simres)
testDispersion(simres)
testZeroInflation(simres) 
car::Anova(mod_TMX_feeds)

mod_TMX_feeds_red <- glmmTMB(number_feedingattempts~Solution + as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "TMX"),])
car::Anova(mod_TMX_feeds_red)

emmb <- emmeans(mod_TMX_feeds, ~ Solution|Time_bin)
contrast(emmb, method = "revpairwise", adjust = "bonferroni")
```


## Scopolamine

```{r}
# Summarize the mean for each Solution in each time bin, filtering for Nic_choice
mean_data_scopolamine_duration <- binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "SCOP"),] %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_fed = mean(total_duration, na.rm = TRUE),
                   se = sd(total_duration, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
                   lower = mean_fed - se,
                   upper = mean_fed + se,
                   .groups = "drop")
mean_data_scopolamine_feeds <- binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "SCOP"),] %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(mean_fed = mean(number_feedingattempts, na.rm = TRUE),
                   se = sd(number_feedingattempts, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
                   lower = mean_fed - se,
                   upper = mean_fed + se,
                   .groups = "drop")

mean_data_scopolamine_duration$midpoint_hrs <- c(2, 2, 6, 6, 10, 10, 14, 14, 18, 18, 22, 22)
mean_data_scopolamine_feeds$midpoint_hrs <- c(2, 2, 6, 6, 10, 10, 14, 14, 18, 18, 22, 22)
```

### Duration

```{r}
# Create the plot
scopolamine_duration_binned_plot <- ggplot(binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "SCOP"),],
                                      aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = total_duration, color = Solution, shape = Solution)) +
  # Scatter plot with jitter for individual points
  geom_point(aes(color = Solution, shape = Solution), alpha = 0.5,  position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0)) + 
  geom_ribbon(data = mean_data_scopolamine_duration, inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(x = midpoint_hrs, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = Solution),
              alpha = 0.4) +
  # Line connecting the mean values across time bins
  geom_line(data = mean_data_scopolamine_duration, aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = mean_fed, color = Solution), size = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "Scopolamine" = "#4B0082")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "Scopolamine" = "#4B0082")) +
  labs(x = "Time segment (h)",
       y = "Total feeding duration (s)",
       title = "") +
  theme_minimal() + scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22), labels = c("0â€“4", "4â€“8", "8â€“12", "12-16", "16-20", "20-24")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(), legend.position = "none", 
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold",)) + ylim(c(0, 300))

scopolamine_duration_binned_plot
```

```{r}
mod_scopolamine_duration <- glmmTMB(total_duration~Solution * as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "SCOP"),])

simres <- simulateResiduals(mod_scopolamine_duration)
plot(simres)
testDispersion(simres)
testZeroInflation(simres) 
car::Anova(mod_scopolamine_duration)

mod_scopolamine_duration_red <- glmmTMB(total_duration~Solution + as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "SCOP"),])
car::Anova(mod_scopolamine_duration_red)

emmb <- emmeans(mod_scopolamine_duration, ~ Solution|Time_bin)
contrast(emmb, method = "pairwise", adjust = "bonferroni")
```

### Feeds

```{r}
# Create the plot
scopolamine_feeds_binned_plot <- ggplot(binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "SCOP"),],
                                      aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = number_feedingattempts, color = Solution, shape = Solution)) +
  # Scatter plot with jitter for individual points
  geom_point(aes(color = Solution, shape = Solution), alpha = 0.5,  position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0)) + 
  geom_ribbon(data = mean_data_scopolamine_feeds, inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(x = midpoint_hrs, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = Solution),
              alpha = 0.4) +
  # Line connecting the mean values across time bins
  geom_line(data = mean_data_scopolamine_feeds, aes(x = midpoint_hrs, y = mean_fed, color = Solution), size = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "Scopolamine" = "#4B0082")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Sucrose" = "#5dade2", "Scopolamine" = "#4B0082")) +
  labs(x = "Time segment (h)",
       y = "Total feeding duration (s)",
       title = "") +
  theme_minimal() + scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22), labels = c("0â€“4", "4â€“8", "8â€“12", "12-16", "16-20", "20-24")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(), legend.position = "none", 
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold",)) 

scopolamine_feeds_binned_plot
```

```{r}
mod_scopolamine_feeds <- glmmTMB(number_feedingattempts~Solution * as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "SCOP"),])

simres <- simulateResiduals(mod_scopolamine_feeds)
plot(simres)
testDispersion(simres)
testZeroInflation(simres) 
car::Anova(mod_scopolamine_feeds)

mod_scopolamine_feeds_red <- glmmTMB(number_feedingattempts~Solution + as.factor(Time_bin) + (1|bee_ID), 
                             family = tweedie(), 
                             data = binned_data_all_summarised[which(binned_data_all_summarised$Treatment == "SCOP"),])
car::Anova(mod_scopolamine_feeds_red)

emmb <- emmeans(mod_scopolamine_feeds, ~ Solution|Time_bin)
contrast(emmb, method = "pairwise", adjust = "bonferroni")
```

**************************************
# 4. FBD analysis
**************************************

# First bout duration analysis

```{r}
filtered_drinking_data <- filtered_drinking_data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Round, RPi) %>%              # Group by Round and RPi
  dplyr::mutate(bee_ID = paste(Round, RPi, sep = "_")) %>%    # Assign a unique ID for each (Round, RPi) combination
  ungroup()

filtered_drinking_data <- filtered_drinking_data %>%
    dplyr::group_by(bee_ID) %>% # Group by Round and RPi
    dplyr::arrange(Time_min) %>% #Order by time
    dplyr::mutate(bout_no_perbee = row_number()) %>% # Create an incremental bout number per bee
    ungroup()

first_bouts <- filtered_drinking_data[which(filtered_drinking_data$Treatment!="SUC"),] %>%
    dplyr::filter(bout_no_perbee == 1)

first_feeding_timestamps <- first_bouts$Time_min

first_bouts$Solution <- factor(first_bouts$Solution, levels = c("Sucrose", "Arecoline", "Caffeine", "TMX", "Scopolamine"))
```


Vs initiation of feeding on second tube

```{r}
# Get the first feeding time for each bee (first feed)
first_feeding_time <- filtered_drinking_data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(bee_ID) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(bee_ID, Time_min) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(start_time = min(Time_min), tube = first(Tube))
```

```{r}
# Find the first switch to the opposite tube for each bee
opposite_feeding_time <- filtered_drinking_data %>%
  dplyr::left_join(first_feeding_time, by = "bee_ID") %>%  # Join to get start_tube for each bee
  dplyr::filter(Time_min > start_time & Tube != tube) %>%  # Filter for opposite tube after first feed
  dplyr::group_by(bee_ID) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    switch_time = min(Time_min),   # Time of the first switch to the opposite tube
    switch_tube = first(Tube))     # The tube fed on after switching

# Combine the results (start time + switch time)
feeding_summary <- first_feeding_time %>%
  dplyr::left_join(opposite_feeding_time, by = "bee_ID")

switch_timestamps <- feeding_summary$switch_time
```

First bouts per bee from both tubes:
```{r}
first_opposite_feeding_bout <- filtered_drinking_data %>%
  dplyr::left_join(first_feeding_time, by = "bee_ID") %>%  # Join to get start_tube for each bee
  dplyr::filter(Time_min > start_time & Tube != tube) %>%  # Filter for opposite tube after first feed
  dplyr::group_by(bee_ID) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Time_min == min(Time_min)) %>%  # Keep only the row with the lowest Time_min per bee_ID
  dplyr::ungroup()  # Ungroup to avoid unintended grouped behavior later

all_first_bouts <- bind_rows(
  first_opposite_feeding_bout %>% select(-bee_ID, -start_time, -tube),  # Remove the three columns
  first_bouts)

all_first_bouts$Solution <- factor(all_first_bouts$Solution, levels = c("Sucrose", "Arecoline", "Caffeine", "TMX", "Scopolamine"))
```

**3a. Duration of first bout**
```{r}
first_bout_duration_analysis <- all_first_bouts %>%
  dplyr::mutate(bee_ID2 = paste(Round, RPi, sep = "_")) %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID2, Treatment, Solution, duration) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Solution,
    values_from = duration,
    names_prefix = "firstbout_"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    diff_firstbout = case_when(
      Treatment == "AREC" ~ firstbout_Arecoline - firstbout_Sucrose,
      Treatment == "CAF" ~ firstbout_Caffeine - firstbout_Sucrose,
      Treatment == "TMX" ~ firstbout_TMX - firstbout_Sucrose,
      Treatment == "SCOP" ~ firstbout_Scopolamine - firstbout_Sucrose,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_))

first_bout_duration_analysis$diff_firstbout_s <- first_bout_duration_analysis$diff_firstbout*60
```

```{r}
# Summarize means and SE per treatment
summary_stats <- first_bout_duration_analysis %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(diff_firstbout_s)),
    mean_diff = mean(diff_firstbout_s, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_diff = sd(diff_firstbout_s, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_diff = sd_diff / sqrt(n))
```


```{r}
# Define custom order
treatment_order <- c("CAF", "SCOP", "TMX", "AREC")
# Apply order to both datasets
summary_stats$Treatment <- factor(summary_stats$Treatment, levels = treatment_order)

summary_stats <- summary_stats %>%
  filter(!is.na(Treatment))

fbd_dif_plot <- ggplot() +
  # Bar for group mean
  geom_col(data = summary_stats,
           aes(x = Treatment, y = mean_diff, fill = Treatment),
           width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8) +
  # Error bars (SE)
  geom_errorbar(data = summary_stats,
                aes(x = Treatment, ymin = mean_diff - se_diff, ymax = mean_diff + se_diff),
                width = 0.2, color = "black") +
  # Horizontal reference line at 0
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey30") +
  # Flip axes to get horizontal bars
  coord_flip() + 
  # Custom fill colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    AREC = "red",  
    TMX = "darkgreen",
    SCOP = "#5dade2",
    CAF = "#5dade2")) +  # green
  # Labels and theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +  theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()) +
  labs(
    y = "Mean difference in first bout duration (s)",
    x = NULL,
    fill = NULL  # remove redundant legend title
  )

fbd_dif_plot
```

Stats
```{r}
fbd_mod <- lm(diff_firstbout_s ~ Treatment, data = first_bout_duration_analysis)

summary(fbd_mod)
anova(fbd_mod)

emm <- emmeans(fbd_mod, ~ Treatment)
emm
test(emm, null = 0)
```

**************************************
# 5. Preference analysis
**************************************

*Continuous*

```{r}
# Create a numeric bee ID variable
all_drinking_data_cumulative_arecoline <- all_drinking_data_cumulative[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative$Treatment == "AREC"),] %>%
  dplyr::mutate(bee_ID_numeric = as.integer(factor(paste(RPi, Round, sep = "_"))))
```

```{r}
# Calculate preference index for each bee
preference_trajectories_arecoline <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_arecoline %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Time_min) %>%  # Ensure data is ordered by time
  dplyr::mutate(
    cumulative_X = cumsum(ifelse(Solution == "Arecoline", duration, 0)),  # Cumulative duration for N
    cumulative_S = cumsum(ifelse(Solution == "Sucrose", duration, 0)),  # Cumulative duration for S
    preference_index = (cumulative_X - cumulative_S) / (cumulative_X + cumulative_S))

preference_trajectories_arecoline[which(is.na(preference_trajectories_arecoline$preference_index)),]$preference_index <- 0
```

Plot duration-based PI
```{r}
preference_plot_arecoline_duration <- ggplot(
  preference_trajectories_arecoline[which(preference_trajectories_arecoline$Time_min <= 1440 & preference_trajectories_arecoline$Time_min!=0),],
  aes(x = Time_hrs, y = preference_index, color = preference_index)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 0.1, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 4)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",      # red for -1
    mid = "grey90",        # neutral at 0
    high = "red",     # green for +1
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Time in experiment (hours)",
    y = "Cumulative preference index") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

preference_plot_arecoline_duration
```

PI duration

```{r}
binned_data_duration_arecoline <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_0[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Treatment == "AREC" & all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Time_hrs <=24),] %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Time_hrs, Solution, seconds_fed, sip_frequency, mean_inter_sip_interval, Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, Cumulative_sips_overall_bee) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Time_bin = cut_width(Time_hrs, width = 1, boundary = 0)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    max_total_duration = max(Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sips = max(Cumulative_sips_overall_bee),
    av_sipfreq = mean(sip_frequency, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_feedingattempts = n(),
    av_inter_sip_interval = mean(mean_inter_sip_interval, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

# Create a reference grid of all combinations
bee_time_solution_grid <- binned_data_duration_arecoline %>%
  distinct(bee_ID, Time_bin, Solution)

# Expand to all combinations of bee_ID x Time_bin x Solution
full_data <- binned_data_duration_arecoline %>%
  distinct(bee_ID) %>%
  crossing(
    Time_bin = unique(binned_data_duration_arecoline$Time_bin),
    Solution = unique(binned_data_duration_arecoline$Solution))

# Join original data into full grid
filled_data <- full_data %>%
  left_join(binned_data_duration_arecoline, by = c("bee_ID", "Time_bin", "Solution")) %>%
  # Order for fill to work properly
  dplyr::arrange(bee_ID, Solution, Time_bin) %>%
  # Fill missing cumulative durations forward within each bee + solution
  group_by(bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_total_duration, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_sips, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(number_feedingattempts, .direction = "down") %>%
  # If it starts with missing (i.e., first bin), set to 0
  dplyr::mutate(max_total_duration = replace_na(max_total_duration, 0),
         max_sips = replace_na(max_sips, 0),
         number_feedingattempts = replace_na(number_feedingattempts, 0)) %>%
   # Step 1: lag previous total duration
  dplyr::mutate(prev_duration = lag(max_total_duration, default = 0),
                # Step 2: check if it increased
                new_attempt = if_else(max_total_duration > prev_duration, 1, 0),
                # Step 3: cumulative count only when duration increases
                cumulative_feedingattempts = cumsum(if_else(new_attempt == 1, number_feedingattempts, 0))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(bee_ID) & bee_ID != "NA_NA") %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
end_cumulative_data <- filled_data[which(filled_data$Time_bin == "(23,24]"),]
end_cumulative_data$Solution <- as.factor(end_cumulative_data$Solution)

end_pref_df_duration <- end_cumulative_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(Time_bin == "(23,24]") %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Solution, max_total_duration) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Solution,
    values_from = max_total_duration,
    names_prefix = "duration_") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PI_duration = (duration_Arecoline - duration_Sucrose) / (duration_Arecoline + duration_Sucrose)) %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, PI_duration)

all_end_pref <- end_pref_df_duration

all_end_pref_arecoline <- all_end_pref %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1_duration = quantile(PI_duration, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3_duration = quantile(PI_duration, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_duration = Q3_duration - Q1_duration,
    is_outlier_duration = PI_duration < (Q1_duration - 1.5 * IQR_duration) | PI_duration > (Q3_duration + 1.5 * IQR_duration))

arecoline_PI_duration_bp <- ggplot(all_end_pref_arecoline, aes(x = "", y = PI_duration)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0, width = 1) +  # Transparent boxplot
  geom_jitter(
    data = filter(all_end_pref_arecoline, !is_outlier_duration),
    aes(color = PI_duration),
    width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3, fill = "black") +  # Mean as black dot
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +  # Baseline at y = 0
  ylim(c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Conventional 24h volume-based PI",
    x = "",
    y = "Preference index") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",   # Blue
    mid = "snow2",     # Neutral
    high = "red",  # Orange
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "PI") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold"))

arecoline_PI_duration_bp
```



### Caffeine

```{r}
# Create a numeric bee ID variable
all_drinking_data_cumulative_caffeine <- all_drinking_data_cumulative[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative$Treatment == "CAF"),] %>%
  dplyr::mutate(bee_ID_numeric = as.integer(factor(paste(RPi, Round, sep = "_"))))
```

```{r}
# Calculate preference index for each bee
preference_trajectories_caffeine <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_caffeine %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Time_min) %>%  # Ensure data is ordered by time
  dplyr::mutate(
    cumulative_X = cumsum(ifelse(Solution == "Caffeine", duration, 0)),  # Cumulative duration for N
    cumulative_S = cumsum(ifelse(Solution == "Sucrose", duration, 0)),  # Cumulative duration for S
    preference_index = (cumulative_X - cumulative_S) / (cumulative_X + cumulative_S))

preference_trajectories_caffeine[which(is.na(preference_trajectories_caffeine$preference_index)),]$preference_index <- 0
```

Plot duration-based PI
```{r}
preference_plot_caffeine_duration <- ggplot(
  preference_trajectories_caffeine[which(preference_trajectories_caffeine$Time_min <= 1440 & preference_trajectories_caffeine$Time_min!=0),],
  aes(x = Time_hrs, y = preference_index, color = preference_index)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 0.1, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 4)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",      # red for -1
    mid = "grey90",        # neutral at 0
    high = "#FFAC1C",     # green for +1
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Time in experiment (hours)",
    y = "Cumulative preference index") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

preference_plot_caffeine_duration
```

PI duration

```{r}
binned_data_duration_caffeine <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_0[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Treatment == "CAF" & all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Time_hrs <=24),] %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Time_hrs, Solution, seconds_fed, sip_frequency, mean_inter_sip_interval, Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, Cumulative_sips_overall_bee) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Time_bin = cut_width(Time_hrs, width = 1, boundary = 0)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    max_total_duration = max(Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sips = max(Cumulative_sips_overall_bee),
    av_sipfreq = mean(sip_frequency, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_feedingattempts = n(),
    av_inter_sip_interval = mean(mean_inter_sip_interval, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

# Create a reference grid of all combinations
bee_time_solution_grid <- binned_data_duration_caffeine %>%
  distinct(bee_ID, Time_bin, Solution)

# Expand to all combinations of bee_ID x Time_bin x Solution
full_data <- binned_data_duration_caffeine %>%
  distinct(bee_ID) %>%
  crossing(
    Time_bin = unique(binned_data_duration_caffeine$Time_bin),
    Solution = unique(binned_data_duration_caffeine$Solution))

# Join original data into full grid
filled_data <- full_data %>%
  left_join(binned_data_duration_caffeine, by = c("bee_ID", "Time_bin", "Solution")) %>%
  # Order for fill to work properly
  dplyr::arrange(bee_ID, Solution, Time_bin) %>%
  # Fill missing cumulative durations forward within each bee + solution
  group_by(bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_total_duration, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_sips, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(number_feedingattempts, .direction = "down") %>%
  # If it starts with missing (i.e., first bin), set to 0
  dplyr::mutate(max_total_duration = replace_na(max_total_duration, 0),
         max_sips = replace_na(max_sips, 0),
         number_feedingattempts = replace_na(number_feedingattempts, 0)) %>%
   # Step 1: lag previous total duration
  dplyr::mutate(prev_duration = lag(max_total_duration, default = 0),
                # Step 2: check if it increased
                new_attempt = if_else(max_total_duration > prev_duration, 1, 0),
                # Step 3: cumulative count only when duration increases
                cumulative_feedingattempts = cumsum(if_else(new_attempt == 1, number_feedingattempts, 0))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(bee_ID) & bee_ID != "NA_NA") %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
end_cumulative_data <- filled_data[which(filled_data$Time_bin == "(23,24]"),]
end_cumulative_data$Solution <- as.factor(end_cumulative_data$Solution)

end_pref_df_duration <- end_cumulative_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(Time_bin == "(23,24]") %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Solution, max_total_duration) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Solution,
    values_from = max_total_duration,
    names_prefix = "duration_") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PI_duration = (duration_Caffeine - duration_Sucrose) / (duration_Caffeine + duration_Sucrose)) %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, PI_duration)

all_end_pref <- end_pref_df_duration

all_end_pref_caffeine <- all_end_pref %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1_duration = quantile(PI_duration, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3_duration = quantile(PI_duration, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_duration = Q3_duration - Q1_duration,
    is_outlier_duration = PI_duration < (Q1_duration - 1.5 * IQR_duration) | PI_duration > (Q3_duration + 1.5 * IQR_duration))

caffeine_PI_duration_bp <- ggplot(all_end_pref_caffeine, aes(x = "", y = PI_duration)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0, width = 1) +  # Transparent boxplot
  geom_jitter(
    data = filter(all_end_pref_caffeine, !is_outlier_duration),
    aes(color = PI_duration),
    width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3, fill = "black") +  # Mean as black dot
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +  # Baseline at y = 0
  ylim(c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Conventional 24h volume-based PI",
    x = "",
    y = "Preference index") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",   # Blue
    mid = "snow2",     # Neutral
    high = "#ffac1c",  # Orange
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "PI") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold"))

caffeine_PI_duration_bp
```



### TMX

```{r}
# Create a numeric bee ID variable
all_drinking_data_cumulative_TMX <- all_drinking_data_cumulative[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative$Treatment == "TMX"),] %>%
  dplyr::mutate(bee_ID_numeric = as.integer(factor(paste(RPi, Round, sep = "_"))))
```

```{r}
# Calculate preference index for each bee
preference_trajectories_TMX <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_TMX %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Time_min) %>%  # Ensure data is ordered by time
  dplyr::mutate(
    cumulative_X = cumsum(ifelse(Solution == "TMX", duration, 0)),  # Cumulative duration for N
    cumulative_S = cumsum(ifelse(Solution == "Sucrose", duration, 0)),  # Cumulative duration for S
    preference_index = (cumulative_X - cumulative_S) / (cumulative_X + cumulative_S))

preference_trajectories_TMX[which(is.na(preference_trajectories_TMX$preference_index)),]$preference_index <- 0
```

Plot duration-based PI
```{r}
preference_plot_TMX_duration <- ggplot(
  preference_trajectories_TMX[which(preference_trajectories_TMX$Time_min <= 1440 & preference_trajectories_TMX$Time_min!=0),],
  aes(x = Time_hrs, y = preference_index, color = preference_index)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 0.1, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 4)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",      # red for -1
    mid = "grey90",        # neutral at 0
    high = "darkgreen",     # green for +1
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Time in experiment (hours)",
    y = "Cumulative preference index") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

preference_plot_TMX_duration
```

PI duration

```{r}
binned_data_duration_tmx <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_0[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Treatment == "TMX" & all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Time_hrs <=24),] %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Time_hrs, Solution, seconds_fed, sip_frequency, mean_inter_sip_interval, Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, Cumulative_sips_overall_bee) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Time_bin = cut_width(Time_hrs, width = 1, boundary = 0)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    max_total_duration = max(Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sips = max(Cumulative_sips_overall_bee),
    av_sipfreq = mean(sip_frequency, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_feedingattempts = n(),
    av_inter_sip_interval = mean(mean_inter_sip_interval, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

# Create a reference grid of all combinations
bee_time_solution_grid <- binned_data_duration_tmx %>%
  distinct(bee_ID, Time_bin, Solution)

# Expand to all combinations of bee_ID x Time_bin x Solution
full_data <- binned_data_duration_tmx %>%
  distinct(bee_ID) %>%
  crossing(
    Time_bin = unique(binned_data_duration_tmx$Time_bin),
    Solution = unique(binned_data_duration_tmx$Solution))

# Join original data into full grid
filled_data <- full_data %>%
  left_join(binned_data_duration_tmx, by = c("bee_ID", "Time_bin", "Solution")) %>%
  # Order for fill to work properly
  dplyr::arrange(bee_ID, Solution, Time_bin) %>%
  # Fill missing cumulative durations forward within each bee + solution
  group_by(bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_total_duration, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_sips, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(number_feedingattempts, .direction = "down") %>%
  # If it starts with missing (i.e., first bin), set to 0
  dplyr::mutate(max_total_duration = replace_na(max_total_duration, 0),
         max_sips = replace_na(max_sips, 0),
         number_feedingattempts = replace_na(number_feedingattempts, 0)) %>%
   # Step 1: lag previous total duration
  dplyr::mutate(prev_duration = lag(max_total_duration, default = 0),
                # Step 2: check if it increased
                new_attempt = if_else(max_total_duration > prev_duration, 1, 0),
                # Step 3: cumulative count only when duration increases
                cumulative_feedingattempts = cumsum(if_else(new_attempt == 1, number_feedingattempts, 0))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(bee_ID) & bee_ID != "NA_NA") %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
end_cumulative_data <- filled_data[which(filled_data$Time_bin == "(23,24]"),]
end_cumulative_data$Solution <- as.factor(end_cumulative_data$Solution)

end_pref_df_duration <- end_cumulative_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(Time_bin == "(23,24]") %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Solution, max_total_duration) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Solution,
    values_from = max_total_duration,
    names_prefix = "duration_") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PI_duration = (duration_TMX - duration_Sucrose) / (duration_TMX + duration_Sucrose)) %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, PI_duration)

all_end_pref <- end_pref_df_duration

all_end_pref_tmx <- all_end_pref %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1_duration = quantile(PI_duration, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3_duration = quantile(PI_duration, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_duration = Q3_duration - Q1_duration,
    is_outlier_duration = PI_duration < (Q1_duration - 1.5 * IQR_duration) | PI_duration > (Q3_duration + 1.5 * IQR_duration))

tmx_PI_duration_bp <- ggplot(all_end_pref_tmx, aes(x = "", y = PI_duration)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0, width = 1) +  # Transparent boxplot
  geom_jitter(
    data = filter(all_end_pref_tmx, !is_outlier_duration),
    aes(color = PI_duration),
    width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3, fill = "black") +  # Mean as black dot
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +  # Baseline at y = 0
  ylim(c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Conventional 24h volume-based PI",
    x = "",
    y = "Preference index") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",   # Blue
    mid = "snow2",     # Neutral
    high = "darkgreen",  # Orange
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "PI") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold"))

tmx_PI_duration_bp
```



### Scopolamine

```{r}
# Create a numeric bee ID variable
all_drinking_data_cumulative_scopolamine <- all_drinking_data_cumulative[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative$Treatment == "SCOP"),] %>%
  dplyr::mutate(bee_ID_numeric = as.integer(factor(paste(RPi, Round, sep = "_"))))
```

```{r}
# Calculate preference index for each bee
preference_trajectories_scopolamine <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_scopolamine %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Time_min) %>%  # Ensure data is ordered by time
  dplyr::mutate(
    cumulative_X = cumsum(ifelse(Solution == "Scopolamine", duration, 0)),  # Cumulative duration for N
    cumulative_S = cumsum(ifelse(Solution == "Sucrose", duration, 0)),  # Cumulative duration for S
    preference_index = (cumulative_X - cumulative_S) / (cumulative_X + cumulative_S))

preference_trajectories_scopolamine[which(is.na(preference_trajectories_scopolamine$preference_index)),]$preference_index <- 0
```

Plot duration-based PI
```{r}
preference_plot_scopolamine_duration <- ggplot(
  preference_trajectories_scopolamine[which(preference_trajectories_scopolamine$Time_min <= 1440 & preference_trajectories_scopolamine$Time_min!=0),],
  aes(x = Time_hrs, y = preference_index, color = preference_index)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 0.1, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 4)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",      # red for -1
    mid = "grey90",        # neutral at 0
    high = "#4B0082",     # green for +1
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Time in experiment (hours)",
    y = "Cumulative preference index") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

preference_plot_scopolamine_duration
```


PI duration

```{r}
binned_data_duration_scopolamine <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_0[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Treatment == "SCOP" & all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Time_hrs <=24),] %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Time_hrs, Solution, seconds_fed, sip_frequency, mean_inter_sip_interval, Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, Cumulative_sips_overall_bee) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Time_bin = cut_width(Time_hrs, width = 1, boundary = 0)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    max_total_duration = max(Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sips = max(Cumulative_sips_overall_bee),
    av_sipfreq = mean(sip_frequency, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_feedingattempts = n(),
    av_inter_sip_interval = mean(mean_inter_sip_interval, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

# Create a reference grid of all combinations
bee_time_solution_grid <- binned_data_duration_scopolamine %>%
  distinct(bee_ID, Time_bin, Solution)

# Expand to all combinations of bee_ID x Time_bin x Solution
full_data <- binned_data_duration_scopolamine %>%
  distinct(bee_ID) %>%
  crossing(
    Time_bin = unique(binned_data_duration_scopolamine$Time_bin),
    Solution = unique(binned_data_duration_scopolamine$Solution))

# Join original data into full grid
filled_data <- full_data %>%
  left_join(binned_data_duration_scopolamine, by = c("bee_ID", "Time_bin", "Solution")) %>%
  # Order for fill to work properly
  dplyr::arrange(bee_ID, Solution, Time_bin) %>%
  # Fill missing cumulative durations forward within each bee + solution
  group_by(bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_total_duration, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_sips, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(number_feedingattempts, .direction = "down") %>%
  # If it starts with missing (i.e., first bin), set to 0
  dplyr::mutate(max_total_duration = replace_na(max_total_duration, 0),
         max_sips = replace_na(max_sips, 0),
         number_feedingattempts = replace_na(number_feedingattempts, 0)) %>%
   # Step 1: lag previous total duration
  dplyr::mutate(prev_duration = lag(max_total_duration, default = 0),
                # Step 2: check if it increased
                new_attempt = if_else(max_total_duration > prev_duration, 1, 0),
                # Step 3: cumulative count only when duration increases
                cumulative_feedingattempts = cumsum(if_else(new_attempt == 1, number_feedingattempts, 0))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(bee_ID) & bee_ID != "NA_NA") %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
end_cumulative_data <- filled_data[which(filled_data$Time_bin == "(23,24]"),]
end_cumulative_data$Solution <- as.factor(end_cumulative_data$Solution)

end_pref_df_duration <- end_cumulative_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(Time_bin == "(23,24]") %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Solution, max_total_duration) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Solution,
    values_from = max_total_duration,
    names_prefix = "duration_") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PI_duration = (duration_Scopolamine - duration_Sucrose) / (duration_Scopolamine + duration_Sucrose)) %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, PI_duration)

all_end_pref <- end_pref_df_duration

all_end_pref_scopolamine <- all_end_pref %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1_duration = quantile(PI_duration, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3_duration = quantile(PI_duration, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_duration = Q3_duration - Q1_duration,
    is_outlier_duration = PI_duration < (Q1_duration - 1.5 * IQR_duration) | PI_duration > (Q3_duration + 1.5 * IQR_duration))

scopolamine_PI_duration_bp <- ggplot(all_end_pref, aes(x = "", y = PI_duration)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0, width = 1) +  # Transparent boxplot
  geom_jitter(
    data = filter(all_end_pref_scopolamine, !is_outlier_duration),
    aes(color = PI_duration),
    width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3, fill = "black") +  # Mean as black dot
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +  # Baseline at y = 0
  ylim(c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Conventional 24h volume-based PI",
    x = "",
    y = "Preference index") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",   # Blue
    mid = "snow2",     # Neutral
    high = "#4b0082",  # Orange
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "PI") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold"))

scopolamine_PI_duration_bp
```


# Mass-based preference

## Mass consumed

```{r}
blinds_ID <- blinds %>%
  dplyr::mutate(bee_ID_numeric = paste(RPi, Round, sep = "_"))

blinds_ID$Change <- as.numeric(blinds_ID$Change)

blinds_ID$Change_net <- blinds_ID$Change - 0.09
blinds_ID$Change_net <- ifelse(blinds_ID$Change_net < 0, 0, blinds_ID$Change_net)
```

### Arecoline

```{r}
# Create a new dataframe with one row per bee and compute the preference index
bee_preference_data_arecoline <- blinds_ID[which(blinds_ID$Treatment == "AREC" & blinds_ID$Notes == "Alive"),] %>%
  dplyr::group_by(bee_ID_numeric) %>%  # Group by bee ID and Treatment
  dplyr::summarize(
    total_X = sum(Change_net[Solution == "Arecoline"], na.rm = TRUE),
    total_S = sum(Change_net[Solution == "Sucrose"], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Calculate preference index based on the treatment
    preference_index = (total_X - total_S) / (total_X + total_S))

bee_preference_data_arecoline <- bee_preference_data_arecoline %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(preference_index, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(preference_index, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = preference_index < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | preference_index > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))
```

Plot mass-based PI

```{r}
arecoline_mass_PI_bp <- ggplot(bee_preference_data_arecoline, aes(x = "", y = preference_index, fill = "")) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0, width = 1) +  # Make boxplots transparent
  geom_jitter(data = filter(bee_preference_data_arecoline, !is_outlier), aes(color = preference_index), width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 1) +  # Add jittered points
  theme_minimal() + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3, color = "black") +  # Mean as a black dot
  labs(
    title = "Conventional 24h volume-based PI",
    x = "",
    y = "Preference index") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",   # Blue
    mid = "snow2",     # Neutral
    high = "red",  # Orange
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "PI") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = "black") +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),  # Align y-axis titles by removing x-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  # y axis label size
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold")) + ylim(c(-1,1))  # Baseline at y=0

arecoline_mass_PI_bp
```

Mass consumed of each solution
```{r}
bee_preference_data_arecoline2 <- bee_preference_data_arecoline %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(preference_index, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(preference_index, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = preference_index < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | preference_index > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))

long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2 <- bee_preference_data_arecoline2 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(total_X, total_S),
    names_to = "Solution",
    names_prefix = "total_",
    values_to = "total")
long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2$Solution <- as.factor(long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2$Solution)

long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2 <- long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Solution) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(total, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(total, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = total < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | total > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))

long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2$ul <- (long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2$total/1.127)*1000
```

```{r}
long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2$Solution <- factor(long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2$Solution, levels = c("S", "X"))

vol_solution_arecoline_boxplots <- ggplot(long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2, aes(x = Solution, y = ul, fill = Solution)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.2, width = 0.5) + #color = "#820427") +  # Make boxplots transparent
  geom_jitter(data = filter(long_bee_preference_data_arecoline2, !is_outlier),
            width = 0.05, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal() + stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3, aes(color = Solution)) +  # Mean as dot
  labs(title = "", x = "", y = "Total consumed (ÎĽl)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("S" = "#5dade2", "X" = "red")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("S" = "#5dade2", "X" = "red")) +  
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),  # Align y-axis titles by removing x-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  # y axis label size
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold")) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("S" = "Sucrose", "X" = "Arecoline")) # Baseline at y=0

vol_solution_arecoline_boxplots
```



### Caffeine

```{r}
# Create a new dataframe with one row per bee and compute the preference index
bee_preference_data_caffeine <- blinds_ID[which(blinds_ID$Treatment == "CAF" & blinds_ID$Notes == "Alive"),] %>%
  dplyr::group_by(bee_ID_numeric) %>%  # Group by bee ID and Treatment
  dplyr::summarize(
    total_X = sum(Change_net[Solution == "Caffeine"], na.rm = TRUE),
    total_S = sum(Change_net[Solution == "Sucrose"], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Calculate preference index based on the treatment
    preference_index = (total_X - total_S) / (total_X + total_S))

bee_preference_data_caffeine <- bee_preference_data_caffeine %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(preference_index, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(preference_index, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = preference_index < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | preference_index > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))
```

Plot mass-based PI

```{r}
caffeine_mass_PI_bp <- ggplot(bee_preference_data_caffeine, aes(x = "", y = preference_index, fill = "")) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0, width = 1) +  # Make boxplots transparent
  geom_jitter(data = filter(bee_preference_data_caffeine, !is_outlier), aes(color = preference_index), width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 1) +  # Add jittered points
  theme_minimal() + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3, color = "black") +  # Mean as a black dot
  labs(
    title = "Conventional 24h volume-based PI",
    x = "",
    y = "Preference index") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",   # Blue
    mid = "snow2",     # Neutral
    high = "#FFAC1C",  # Orange
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "PI") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = "black") +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),  # Align y-axis titles by removing x-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  # y axis label size
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold")) + ylim(c(-1,1))  # Baseline at y=0

caffeine_mass_PI_bp
```

Mass consumed of each solution
```{r}
bee_preference_data_caffeine2 <- bee_preference_data_caffeine %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(preference_index, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(preference_index, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = preference_index < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | preference_index > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))

long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2 <- bee_preference_data_caffeine2 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(total_X, total_S),
    names_to = "Solution",
    names_prefix = "total_",
    values_to = "total")
long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2$Solution <- as.factor(long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2$Solution)

long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2 <- long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Solution) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(total, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(total, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = total < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | total > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))

long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2$ul <- (long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2$total/1.127)*1000
```

```{r}
long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2$Solution <- factor(long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2$Solution, levels = c("S", "X"))

vol_solution_caffeine_boxplots <- ggplot(long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2, aes(x = Solution, y = ul, fill = Solution)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.2, width = 0.5) + #color = "#820427") +  # Make boxplots transparent
  geom_jitter(data = filter(long_bee_preference_data_caffeine2, !is_outlier),
            width = 0.05, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal() + stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3, aes(color = Solution)) +  # Mean as dot
  labs(title = "", x = "", y = "Total consumed (ÎĽl)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("S" = "#5dade2", "X" = "#FFAC1C")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("S" = "#5dade2", "X" = "#FFAC1C")) +  
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),  # Align y-axis titles by removing x-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  # y axis label size
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold")) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("S" = "Sucrose", "X" = "Caffeine")) # Baseline at y=0

vol_solution_caffeine_boxplots
```


### TMX

```{r}
# Create a new dataframe with one row per bee and compute the preference index
bee_preference_data_TMX <- blinds_ID[which(blinds_ID$Treatment == "TMX" & blinds_ID$Notes == "Alive"),] %>%
  dplyr::group_by(bee_ID_numeric) %>%  # Group by bee ID and Treatment
  dplyr::summarize(
    total_X = sum(Change_net[Solution == "TMX"], na.rm = TRUE),
    total_S = sum(Change_net[Solution == "Sucrose"], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Calculate preference index based on the treatment
    preference_index = (total_X - total_S) / (total_X + total_S))

bee_preference_data_TMX <- bee_preference_data_TMX %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(preference_index, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(preference_index, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = preference_index < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | preference_index > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))
```

Plot mass-based PI

```{r}
TMX_mass_PI_bp <- ggplot(bee_preference_data_TMX, aes(x = "", y = preference_index, fill = "")) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0, width = 1) +  # Make boxplots transparent
  geom_jitter(data = filter(bee_preference_data_TMX, !is_outlier), aes(color = preference_index), width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 1) +  # Add jittered points
  theme_minimal() + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3, color = "black") +  # Mean as a black dot
  labs(
    title = "Conventional 24h volume-based PI",
    x = "",
    y = "Preference index") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",   # Blue
    mid = "snow2",     # Neutral
    high = "darkgreen",  # Orange
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "PI") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = "black") +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),  # Align y-axis titles by removing x-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  # y axis label size
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold")) + ylim(c(-1,1))  # Baseline at y=0

TMX_mass_PI_bp
```

Mass consumed of each solution
```{r}
bee_preference_data_TMX2 <- bee_preference_data_TMX %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(preference_index, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(preference_index, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = preference_index < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | preference_index > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))

long_bee_preference_data_TMX2 <- bee_preference_data_TMX2 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(total_X, total_S),
    names_to = "Solution",
    names_prefix = "total_",
    values_to = "total")
long_bee_preference_data_TMX2$Solution <- as.factor(long_bee_preference_data_TMX2$Solution)

long_bee_preference_data_TMX2 <- long_bee_preference_data_TMX2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Solution) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(total, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(total, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = total < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | total > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))

long_bee_preference_data_TMX2$ul <- (long_bee_preference_data_TMX2$total/1.127)*1000
```

```{r}
long_bee_preference_data_TMX2$Solution <- factor(long_bee_preference_data_TMX2$Solution, levels = c("S", "X"))

vol_solution_TMX_boxplots <- ggplot(long_bee_preference_data_TMX2, aes(x = Solution, y = ul, fill = Solution)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.2, width = 0.5) + #color = "#820427") +  # Make boxplots transparent
  geom_jitter(data = filter(long_bee_preference_data_TMX2, !is_outlier),
            width = 0.05, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal() + stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3, aes(color = Solution)) +  # Mean as dot
  labs(title = "", x = "", y = "Total consumed (ÎĽl)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("S" = "#5dade2", "X" = "darkgreen")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("S" = "#5dade2", "X" = "darkgreen")) +  
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),  # Align y-axis titles by removing x-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  # y axis label size
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold")) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("S" = "Sucrose", "X" = "TMX")) # Baseline at y=0

vol_solution_TMX_boxplots
```


### Scopolamine

```{r}
# Create a new dataframe with one row per bee and compute the preference index
bee_preference_data_scopolamine <- blinds_ID[which(blinds_ID$Treatment == "SCOP" & blinds_ID$Notes == "Alive"),] %>%
  dplyr::group_by(bee_ID_numeric) %>%  # Group by bee ID and Treatment
  dplyr::summarize(
    total_X = sum(Change_net[Solution == "Scopolamine"], na.rm = TRUE),
    total_S = sum(Change_net[Solution == "Sucrose"], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Calculate preference index based on the treatment
    preference_index = (total_X - total_S) / (total_X + total_S))

bee_preference_data_scopolamine <- bee_preference_data_scopolamine %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(preference_index, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(preference_index, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = preference_index < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | preference_index > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))
```

Plot mass-based PI

```{r}
scopolamine_mass_PI_bp <- ggplot(bee_preference_data_scopolamine, aes(x = "", y = preference_index, fill = "")) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0, width = 1) +  # Make boxplots transparent
  geom_jitter(data = filter(bee_preference_data_scopolamine, !is_outlier), aes(color = preference_index), width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 1) +  # Add jittered points
  theme_minimal() + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3, color = "black") +  # Mean as a black dot
  labs(
    title = "Conventional 24h volume-based PI",
    x = "",
    y = "Preference index") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#5dade2",   # Blue
    mid = "snow2",     # Neutral
    high = "#4B0082",  # Orange
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    name = "PI") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = "black") +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),  # Align y-axis titles by removing x-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  # y axis label size
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold")) + ylim(c(-1,1))  # Baseline at y=0

scopolamine_mass_PI_bp
```

Mass consumed of each solution
```{r}
bee_preference_data_scopolamine2 <- bee_preference_data_scopolamine %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(preference_index, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(preference_index, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = preference_index < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | preference_index > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))

long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2 <- bee_preference_data_scopolamine2 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(total_X, total_S),
    names_to = "Solution",
    names_prefix = "total_",
    values_to = "total")
long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2$Solution <- as.factor(long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2$Solution)

long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2 <- long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Solution) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Q1 = quantile(total, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(total, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    is_outlier = total < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | total > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))

long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2$ul <- (long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2$total/1.127)*1000
```

```{r}
long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2$Solution <- factor(long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2$Solution, levels = c("S", "X"))

vol_solution_scopolamine_boxplots <- ggplot(long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2, aes(x = Solution, y = ul, fill = Solution)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.2, width = 0.5) + #color = "#820427") +  # Make boxplots transparent
  geom_jitter(data = filter(long_bee_preference_data_scopolamine2, !is_outlier),
            width = 0.05, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal() + stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3, aes(color = Solution)) +  # Mean as dot
  labs(title = "", x = "", y = "Total consumed (ÎĽl)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("S" = "#5dade2", "X" = "#4B0082")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("S" = "#5dade2", "X" = "#4B0082")) +  
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 0),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 0),  # Align y-axis titles by removing x-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  # y axis label size
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),  # Transparent panel background
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),    # Transparent plot background
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 0, face = "bold")) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey", size = 0.8) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("S" = "Sucrose", "X" = "Scopolamine")) # Baseline at y=0

vol_solution_scopolamine_boxplots
```

Statistical analysis

```{r}
bee_preference_data_arecoline$bee_ID <- bee_preference_data_arecoline$bee_ID_numeric
merged_df_arecoline <- dplyr::left_join(all_end_pref_arecoline, bee_preference_data_arecoline, by = "bee_ID")
merged_df_arecoline2 <- merged_df_arecoline %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    PI_duration = PI_duration,
    PI_mass = preference_index) %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, PI_duration, PI_mass)

bee_preference_data_caffeine$bee_ID <- bee_preference_data_caffeine$bee_ID_numeric
merged_df_caffeine <- dplyr::left_join(all_end_pref_caffeine, bee_preference_data_caffeine, by = "bee_ID")
merged_df_caffeine2 <- merged_df_caffeine %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    PI_duration = PI_duration,
    PI_mass = preference_index) %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, PI_duration, PI_mass)

bee_preference_data_TMX$bee_ID <- bee_preference_data_TMX$bee_ID_numeric
merged_df_TMX <- dplyr::left_join(all_end_pref_tmx, bee_preference_data_TMX, by = "bee_ID")
merged_df_TMX2 <- merged_df_TMX %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    PI_duration = PI_duration,
    PI_mass = preference_index) %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, PI_duration, PI_mass)

bee_preference_data_scopolamine$bee_ID <- bee_preference_data_scopolamine$bee_ID_numeric
merged_df_scopolamine <- dplyr::left_join(all_end_pref_scopolamine, bee_preference_data_scopolamine, by = "bee_ID")
merged_df_scopolamine2 <- merged_df_scopolamine %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    PI_duration = PI_duration,
    PI_mass = preference_index) %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, PI_duration, PI_mass)

# Add a treatment column to each dataframe
merged_df_arecoline2 <- merged_df_arecoline2 %>%
  dplyr::mutate(treatment = "AREC")
merged_df_caffeine2 <- merged_df_caffeine2 %>%
  dplyr::mutate(treatment = "CAF")
merged_df_TMX2 <- merged_df_TMX2 %>%
  dplyr::mutate(treatment = "TMX")
merged_df_scopolamine2 <- merged_df_scopolamine2 %>%
  dplyr::mutate(treatment = "SCOP")
# Combine all
combined <- bind_rows(merged_df_arecoline2, merged_df_caffeine2, merged_df_TMX2, merged_df_scopolamine2)
# Pivot longer to create a 'type' and 'PI' column
long_combined <- combined %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(PI_duration, PI_mass),
               names_to = "type",
               values_to = "PI") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(type = recode(type,
                       PI_duration = "duration",
                       PI_mass = "mass"))
```

```{r}
long_combined$type <- as.factor(long_combined$type)
long_combined$treatment <- as.factor(long_combined$treatment)

# Mixed model with random intercepts for bee_ID
library(lmerTest)
mod <- lmer(PI ~ treatment * type + (1 | bee_ID), data = long_combined)
anova(mod)

# Estimated marginal means
emm <- emmeans(mod, ~ treatment * type)
contrast(emm, method = "eff", adjust = "none")
contrast(emm, method = "eff", adjust = "fdr")
```

**************************************
# 6. Individual trajectories
**************************************

```{r}
binned_data_duration_arecoline <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_0[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Treatment == "AREC" & all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Time_hrs <=24),] %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Time_hrs, Solution, seconds_fed, sip_frequency, mean_inter_sip_interval, Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, Cumulative_sips_overall_bee) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Time_bin = cut_width(Time_hrs, width = 1, boundary = 0)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    max_total_duration = max(Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sips = max(Cumulative_sips_overall_bee),
    av_sipfreq = mean(sip_frequency, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_feedingattempts = n(),
    av_inter_sip_interval = mean(mean_inter_sip_interval, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

binned_data_duration_caffeine <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_0[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Treatment == "CAF" & all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Time_hrs <=24),] %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Time_hrs, Solution, seconds_fed, sip_frequency, mean_inter_sip_interval, Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, Cumulative_sips_overall_bee) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Time_bin = cut_width(Time_hrs, width = 1, boundary = 0)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    max_total_duration = max(Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sips = max(Cumulative_sips_overall_bee),
    av_sipfreq = mean(sip_frequency, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_feedingattempts = n(),
    av_inter_sip_interval = mean(mean_inter_sip_interval, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

binned_data_duration_nic_TMX <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_0[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Treatment == "TMX" & all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Time_hrs <=24),] %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Time_hrs, Solution, seconds_fed, sip_frequency, mean_inter_sip_interval, Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, Cumulative_sips_overall_bee) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Time_bin = cut_width(Time_hrs, width = 1, boundary = 0)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    max_total_duration = max(Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sips = max(Cumulative_sips_overall_bee),
    av_sipfreq = mean(sip_frequency, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_feedingattempts = n(),
    av_inter_sip_interval = mean(mean_inter_sip_interval, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

binned_data_duration_scopolamine <- all_drinking_data_cumulative_0[which(all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Treatment == "SCOP" & all_drinking_data_cumulative_0$Time_hrs <=24),] %>%
  dplyr::select(bee_ID, Time_hrs, Solution, seconds_fed, sip_frequency, mean_inter_sip_interval, Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, Cumulative_sips_overall_bee) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Time_bin = cut_width(Time_hrs, width = 1, boundary = 0)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin, bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    max_total_duration = max(Cumulative_indiv_duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sips = max(Cumulative_sips_overall_bee),
    av_sipfreq = mean(sip_frequency, na.rm = TRUE),
    number_feedingattempts = n(),
    av_inter_sip_interval = mean(mean_inter_sip_interval, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")
```


## Arecoline

Fill in missing cumulative entries (if no bouts occurred for a given bee in a given time bin, add a row to the dataframe with 0s for the behaviours)
```{r}
# Create a reference grid of all combinations
bee_time_solution_grid_arecoline <- binned_data_duration_arecoline %>%
  distinct(bee_ID, Time_bin, Solution)

# Expand to all combinations of bee_ID x Time_bin x Solution
full_data_arecoline <- binned_data_duration_arecoline %>%
  distinct(bee_ID) %>%
  crossing(
    Time_bin = unique(binned_data_duration_arecoline$Time_bin),
    Solution = unique(binned_data_duration_arecoline$Solution))

# Join original data into full grid
filled_data_arecoline <- full_data_arecoline %>%
  left_join(binned_data_duration_arecoline, by = c("bee_ID", "Time_bin", "Solution")) %>%
  # Order for fill to work properly
  dplyr::arrange(bee_ID, Solution, Time_bin) %>%
  # Fill missing cumulative durations forward within each bee + solution
  group_by(bee_ID, Solution) %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_total_duration, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(max_sips, .direction = "down") %>%
  tidyr::fill(number_feedingattempts, .direction = "down") %>%
  # If it starts with missing (i.e., first bin), set to 0
  dplyr::mutate(max_total_duration = replace_na(max_total_duration, 0),
         max_sips = replace_na(max_sips, 0),
         number_feedingattempts = replace_na(number_feedingattempts, 0)) %>%
   # Step 1: lag previous total duration
  dplyr::mutate(prev_duration = lag(max_total_duration, default = 0),
                # Step 2: check if it increased
                new_attempt = if_else(max_total_duration > prev_duration, 1, 0),
                # Step 3: cumulative count only when duration increases
                cumulative_feedingattempts = cumsum(if_else(new_attempt == 1, number_feedingattempts, 0))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(bee_ID) & bee_ID != "NA_NA") %>%
  ungroup()
```

Average cumulative duration, sips, and attempts within time bins and solutions

```{r}
binned_data_summary_arecoline <- filled_data_arecoline %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Time_bin_str = as.character(Time_bin),
    Time_bin_upper = as.numeric(sub(".*,(.*)\\)|.*,(.*)\\]", "\\1\\2", Time_bin_str))) 

binned_data_summary_arecoline <- binned_data_summary_arecoline %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Solution, Time_bin_upper) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Time_bin_upper, Solution) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(av_cumulative_duration = mean(max_total_duration),
                   av_cumulative_sips = mean(max_sips),
                   av_sip_frequency = mean(av_sipfreq, na.rm = TRUE),
                   av_cumulative_attempts = mean(cumulative_feedingattempts),
                   av_ISI = mean(av_inter_sip_interval),
                   .groups = "drop")
  
new_row <- data.frame(
  Time_bin_upper = 0,
  Solution = c("Arecoline", "Sucrose"),  # Add both solutions for Time_bin = 0
  av_cumulative_duration = 0,
  av_cumulative_sips = 0,
  av_cumulative_attempts = 0,
  av_sip_frequency = 0,
  av_ISI = 0)

binned_data_summary_arecoline <- bind_rows(binned_data_summary_arecoline, new_row) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Solution, Time_bin_upper)

binned_data_summary_arecoline <- binned_data_summary_arecoline %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Solution, Time_bin_upper) %>%  # Ensure the data is ordered by Solution and Time_bin_upper
  dplyr::group_by(Solution) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    av_duration_change = av_cumulative_duration - lag(av_cumulative_duration), #Do this for rate analysis 
    av_sips_change = av_cumulative_sips - lag(av_cumulative_sips),
    av_attempts_change = av_cumulative_attempts - lag(av_cumulative_attempts)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(av_duration_change = replace_na(av_duration_change, 0),
                av_sips_change = replace_na(av_sips_change, 0),
                av_attempts_change = replace_na(av_attempts_change, 0)) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
filled_data_arecoline <- filled_data_arecoline %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    total_seconds_fed = dplyr::if_else(
      Time_bin == "[0,1]",
      max_total_duration,
      max_total_duration - prev_duration))

filled_data_4h_arecoline <- filled_data_arecoline %>%
  # Extract lower bound of each bin
  dplyr::mutate(hour_start = as.numeric(str_extract(Time_bin, "\\d+"))) %>%
  # Create 4 h bins
  dplyr::mutate(
    bin_4h_start = floor(hour_start / 4) * 4,
      Time_bin_minutes = paste0(
      bin_4h_start * 60,
      "-",
      (bin_4h_start + 4) * 60)) %>%
  # Sum feeding time within 4 h bins
  dplyr::group_by(bee_ID, Solution, Time_bin_minutes) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    total_seconds_fed = sum(total_seconds_fed, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop")

filled_data_4h_arecoline <- filled_data_4h_arecoline %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    bin_start_min = as.numeric(str_extract(Time_bin_minutes, "^\\d+"))) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(bee_ID, Solution, bin_start_min)
```

```{r}
final_totals_arecoline <- filled_data_4h_arecoline %>%
```
